metadata.title: Edge Cases: Very Long Captions, Many References, Lists, and Nested Structure

affiliations.department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering

metadata.abstract: This fixture stress-tests edge cases in the IEEE compiler: (1) multiple authors with shared affiliations, (2) very long figure and table captions that wrap across multiple lines, (3) bullet and numbered lists, (4) three heading levels, (5) a large reference list with 10 entries covering journals, conferences, books, and online sources. This tests caption line wrapping, hanging indent in references, and correct IEEE heading numbering at all three levels.
metadata.conference: 2025 IEEE Conference on Compiler Design for Academic Publishing

blocks.text: This document exercises edge cases that are most likely to cause layout problems in a typesetting pipeline. We systematically test each class of edge case in separate sections.

items: Long captions that wrap across multiple lines in a narrow IEEE column
Three levels of section headings (I, I.A, I.A.1)
Bullet and numbered lists with multiple items
References with DOIs, page ranges, and URLs

blocks.text: Each test case is rendered in isolation and inspected visually. Automated checks verify that images are present, styles are applied correctly, and no paragraphs overflow the column boundaries.
The following table has a very long caption designed to wrap multiple lines within a single column width, testing that the caption style handles word-wrap correctly without overflow or truncation.

blocks.caption: Detailed comparison of rendering accuracy, processing latency, memory consumption, and output quality scores across seven different algorithmic configurations tested on the IEEE document compilation benchmark dataset, evaluated on both A4 and Letter page sizes.

items: Initialize the document model from the JSON fixture.
Run preflight checks on all referenced image files.
Render any equation blocks to PNG using the math backend.
Build the DOCX file using the DocxBuilder class.
Convert the DOCX to PDF using LibreOffice headless.
Perform post-render preflight checks on the resulting PDF.

blocks.text: All edge cases have been included in this fixture. The rendering pipeline should handle each case gracefully, producing a correctly formatted IEEE document without crashes, missing content, or style errors.

Column Layout Algorithms for Two-Column Academic Journals
